architectureOverviewParagraphs: This document describes the target architecture, key components, database design, APIs, integrations, and security design for the WAI Industries Client Portal.
architectureDecisionsNumbered: Use role-based access control (RBAC) with separate client and PRA staff roles.
Store uploaded documents in object storage and metadata in the database.
Expose REST endpoints for submissions, status updates, and document retrieval.

designComponents.name: API Gateway
designComponents.responsibility: Routes requests, enforces authentication, rate limits, and request validation.

designComponents.name: Submission Service
designComponents.responsibility: Creates submissions, tracks status, and manages document metadata.

databaseSchemaIntroParagraphs: The portal stores core entities including users, submissions, and submission documents. Audit logs capture administrative actions for compliance.

apiEndpoints.description: Create a new submission for a selected tax year.
Fetch a submission including its status timeline.

integrationIntroParagraphs: The system integrates with external identity verification and outbound email delivery services.
integrationPointsBullets: SMTP or email provider API for notifications.
securityArchitectureParagraphs: Security controls include authentication, authorization, encryption, and audit logging.
securityAuthParagraphs: Users authenticate with email and password, with optional MFA depending on client policy.
securityAuthorizationParagraphs: Authorization is enforced via RBAC and resource ownership checks on submission resources.
securityDataProtectionParagraphs: All traffic uses TLS. Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest. Uploaded documents are stored with restricted access policies.
securityAuditLoggingParagraphs: Administrative actions are recorded with actor identity, timestamp, target entity, and action type.
